ucs.title: [LLM] FIN4 stealth inbox rule hiding incident-response & M&A correspondence
ucs.description: FIN4's hallmark post-compromise behaviour is to log into the victim's Outlook/OWA with phished credentials and immediately create inbox rules that auto-delete or move security-incident replies (so the real user never sees the warning) and silently forward messages containing M&A / earnings / SEC keywords. This is the single most distinctive non-malware signal of G0085 because they deploy NO implants — only mailbox rules.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(All_Changes.user) as user values(All_Changes.src) as src values(All_Changes.command) as command from datamodel=Change where All_Changes.object_category="mailbox" (All_Changes.action="created" OR All_Changes.action="modified") (All_Changes.command="New-InboxRule" OR All_Changes.command="Set-InboxRule" OR All_Changes.command="UpdateInboxRules") by All_Changes.dest All_Changes.command All_Changes.user _raw | rename All_Changes.* as * | search _raw="*DeleteMessage*" OR _raw="*MoveToFolder*" OR _raw="*ForwardTo*" OR _raw="*RedirectTo*" OR _raw="*ForwardAsAttachmentTo*" OR _raw="*MarkAsRead*" | search _raw="*hacked*" OR _raw="*phish*" OR _raw="*malware*" OR _raw="*virus*" OR _raw="*merger*" OR _raw="*acquisition*" OR _raw="*acquire*" OR _raw="*buyout*" OR _raw="*insider*" OR _raw="*SEC*" OR _raw="*10-K*" OR _raw="*10-Q*" OR _raw="*earnings*" OR _raw="*confidential*" OR _raw="*deal*" | `convert_timeformat`
ucs.kql:
// FIN4 signature: stealth inbox rule + finance/IR keyword target
let _action_kw = dynamic(["DeleteMessage","MoveToFolder","ForwardTo","RedirectTo","ForwardAsAttachmentTo","MarkAsRead"]);
let _topic_kw  = dynamic(["hacked","phishing","phish","malware","virus","compromise",
                          "merger","acquisition","acquire","buyout","insider",
                          "SEC ","10-K","10-Q","earnings","confidential","deal"]);
CloudAppEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where Application == "Microsoft Exchange Online"
| where ActionType in~ ("New-InboxRule","Set-InboxRule","UpdateInboxRules","Set-Mailbox")
| extend Raw = tostring(RawEventData)
| where Raw has_any (_action_kw)
| where Raw has_any (_topic_kw)
| extend RuleName = tostring(parse_json(RawEventData).Parameters)
| project Timestamp, AccountDisplayName, AccountObjectId, ActionType,
          IPAddress, CountryCode, ISP, IsAnonymousProxy, UserAgent,
          RuleParameters = RuleName, Raw
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// FIN4 signature: stealth inbox rule + finance/IR keyword target
let _action_kw = dynamic(["DeleteMessage","MoveToFolder","ForwardTo","RedirectTo","ForwardAsAttachmentTo","MarkAsRead"]);
let _topic_kw  = dynamic(["hacked","phishing","phish","malware","virus","compromise",
                          "merger","acquisition","acquire","buyout","insider",
                          "SEC ","10-K","10-Q","earnings","confidential","deal"]);
OfficeActivity
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where OfficeWorkload == "Exchange"
| where Operation in~ ("New-InboxRule","Set-InboxRule","UpdateInboxRules","Set-Mailbox")
| where ResultStatus in~ ("Succeeded","True","PartiallySucceeded")
| extend ParamStr = tostring(Parameters)
| where ParamStr has_any (_action_kw)        // hide / forward action
| where ParamStr has_any (_topic_kw)         // FIN4-relevant topic
| project TimeGenerated, UserId, MailboxOwnerUPN, ClientIP, UserAgent,
          Operation, ResultStatus, ParamStr
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: FIN4 Stealth Inbox Rule Targeting M&A or Incident-Response Keywords
id: 1f4e5a30-fin4-inbox-rule
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects FIN4 (G0085) post-compromise behaviour where a phished mailbox owner
  has an inbox rule created that auto-deletes or silently forwards messages
  matching incident-response ("hacked", "phishing") or M&A ("merger",
  "acquisition", "SEC", "10-K") keywords. FIN4 deploys no malware - mailbox
  rules are the implant.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0085/
  - https://www.mandiant.com/resources/blog/hacking-the-street-fin4-likely-playing-the-market
author: SOC Detection
date: 2026/05/03
logsource:
  product: m365
  service: exchange
detection:
  selection_op:
    Operation:
      - 'New-InboxRule'
      - 'Set-InboxRule'
      - 'UpdateInboxRules'
  selection_action:
    Parameters|contains:
      - 'DeleteMessage'
      - 'MoveToFolder'
      - 'ForwardTo'
      - 'RedirectTo'
      - 'ForwardAsAttachmentTo'
      - 'MarkAsRead'
  selection_keyword:
    Parameters|contains:
      - 'hacked'
      - 'phish'
      - 'malware'
      - 'virus'
      - 'merger'
      - 'acquisition'
      - 'acquire'
      - 'buyout'
      - 'insider'
      - 'SEC'
      - '10-K'
      - '10-Q'
      - 'earnings'
      - 'confidential'
  condition: all of selection_*
falsepositives:
  - Executive-assistant rules that legitimately filter financial correspondence
  - Compliance teams routing security-related mail
level: high
tags:
  - attack.collection
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.t1114.002
  - attack.t1564.008
  - attack.g0085
ucs.rationale: Mandiant/FireEye's 2014 reporting on FIN4 documents this exact tradecraft: after harvesting OWA credentials they did NOT install malware. Instead they created mailbox rules that auto-deleted any inbound replies containing words like 'hacked' or 'phishing' so the legitimate owner never noticed account abuse, and they monitored mailboxes for words like 'merger', '10-K', 'SEC' for insider-trading mat
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] FIN4 OWA/Exchange Online access via Tor/anonymising proxy with legacy mail protocol
ucs.description: Once FIN4 has phished credentials they log directly into Outlook Web Access or Exchange Online to read mail, and FireEye observed them routing this access through Tor (T1090.003) to obscure origin. This UC catches successful sign-ins to mail workloads from anonymising proxies, especially when paired with legacy auth (IMAP/POP/SMTP/'Other clients') which bypasses MFA - exactly how FIN4 maintains low-footprint persistent mailbox access.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Authentication.app) as app values(Authentication.src) as src values(Authentication.user_agent) as user_agent from datamodel=Authentication where Authentication.action="success" (Authentication.app="*Exchange*" OR Authentication.app="*Outlook*" OR Authentication.app="*OWA*") by Authentication.user Authentication.src _time span=1h | rename Authentication.* as * | lookup tor_exit_nodes ip as src OUTPUT is_tor | lookup anonymizing_proxy ip as src OUTPUT is_anon_proxy | where is_tor="true" OR is_anon_proxy="true" OR match(user_agent,"(?i)IMAP|POP3|SMTP|Other clients") | stats min(firstTime) as firstSeen max(lastTime) as lastSeen values(app) as apps values(src) as src_ips values(user_agent) as agents by user | `convert_timeformat`
ucs.kql:
// FIN4: Tor / anonymising-proxy access to mail workloads, esp. via legacy auth
let _mail_apps = dynamic(["Office 365 Exchange Online","Exchange Online",
                          "Outlook","Outlook Mobile","Outlook Web Access","OWA",
                          "Microsoft Exchange REST API Based Powershell",
                          "Exchange ActiveSync"]);
let _legacy = dynamic(["Other clients","IMAP4","POP3","Authenticated SMTP",
                       "Exchange ActiveSync","Exchange Online PowerShell"]);
AADSignInEventsBeta
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where ErrorCode == 0
| where Application in~ (_mail_apps) or ResourceDisplayName in~ (_mail_apps)
| where IsAnonymousProxy == true
      or ClientAppUsed in~ (_legacy)
      or RiskLevelDuringSignIn in~ ("medium","high")
| extend Indicator = case(IsAnonymousProxy == true, "AnonProxy/Tor",
                          ClientAppUsed in~ (_legacy), strcat("LegacyAuth:",ClientAppUsed),
                          "RiskySignIn")
| project Timestamp, AccountUpn, AccountDisplayName, IPAddress, Country, City,
          Application, ResourceDisplayName, ClientAppUsed, UserAgent,
          IsAnonymousProxy, RiskLevelDuringSignIn, Indicator
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// FIN4: Tor / anonymising-proxy access to mail workloads, esp. via legacy auth
let _mail_apps = dynamic(["Office 365 Exchange Online","Exchange Online",
                          "Outlook","Outlook Mobile","Outlook Web Access","OWA",
                          "Microsoft Exchange REST API Based Powershell",
                          "Exchange ActiveSync"]);
let _legacy = dynamic(["Other clients","IMAP4","POP3","Authenticated SMTP",
                       "Exchange ActiveSync","Exchange Online PowerShell"]);
union isfuzzy=true
  ( SigninLogs
      | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) ),
  ( AADNonInteractiveUserSignInLogs
      | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d) )
| where ResultType == 0
| where AppDisplayName in~ (_mail_apps) or ResourceDisplayName in~ (_mail_apps)
| extend Country = tostring(parse_json(LocationDetails).countryOrRegion),
         City    = tostring(parse_json(LocationDetails).city),
         Risk    = tostring(RiskEventTypes_V2)
| where Risk has_any ("anonymizedIPAddress","tor","anonymousIp","unfamiliarFeatures")
      or ClientAppUsed in~ (_legacy)
      or RiskLevelDuringSignIn in~ ("medium","high")
| extend Indicator = case(Risk has_any ("anonymizedIPAddress","tor","anonymousIp"), "AnonProxy/Tor",
                          ClientAppUsed in~ (_legacy), strcat("LegacyAuth:",ClientAppUsed),
                          "RiskySignIn")
| project TimeGenerated, UserPrincipalName, IPAddress, Country, City,
          AppDisplayName, ResourceDisplayName, ClientAppUsed, UserAgent,
          RiskLevelDuringSignIn, Risk, Indicator
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.rationale: FireEye's FIN4 reporting noted the actor accessed compromised mailboxes from Tor exit nodes and avoided dropping any binary so endpoint signals are absent. Pairing the AAD anonymous-proxy / Tor signal specifically with mail-workload appIds (Exchange Online, OWA, EWS) and legacy-auth client types (IMAP/POP/Other clients) is what discriminates FIN4-style insider-info collection from generic risky si
